original_abstract: This research explores the roles of discipline and religious values in shaping the resilience and responsibility of students at SMP Nidaul Quran in Kabupaten Sarolangun. Employing a qualitative approach and a case study design, data were gathered through interviews, observations, and document analysis. The findings indicate that discipline plays a crucial role in molding students' character, emphasizing an educational approach focused on character development. SMP Nidaul Quran successfully aligns discipline with religious values, cultivating students who are resilient and responsible. The role of teachers as moral guides and the active support of parents are also crucial factors. This research contributes to the development of knowledge and practices in Islamic education. The integration of religious values not only produces academically competent students but also in stills strong moral integrity. The holistic approach involving teachers, parents, and religious values serves as a model for other Islamic educational institutions. These practices promote the understanding that Islamic education shapes the hearts and souls of students to be resilient, responsible, and prepared to face life's challenges.
humanized_abstract: The title of this research is Discipline and Religious Values and Students’ Resilience and Responsibility at SMP Nidaul Quran Kabupaten Sarolangun. This research aims to find out how discipline and religious values affect the resilience and responsibility of students in SMP Nidaul Quran Kabupaten Sarolangun. This research was a qualitative research that used case study. The data was collected by interviewing the students, teachers and the headmaster, by observing and by analyzing the documents available. The result of the research showed that the discipline at SMP Nidaul Quran aimed to form the students’ characters. Therefore, the concept of discipline in this school was used in connection with the concept of character building education. The discipline that is united with religious values is also believed to be able to form students who have resilience and responsibility. In addition, teachers are expected to be moral teachers and the parents are also expected to cooperate actively. It is expected that the results of this research can be very useful for researchers in the field of Islamic education, so that the results can develop the theory and the practice of Islamic education. It is also expected that the values of religion can produce students who are not only competent and successful in academic achievement but are also of high moral values, and therefore this research on the role of teachers and parents in the values of religion at SMP Nidaul Quran can be used as a model for other schools of Islamic education in Indonesia.
